title: cordyla madagascariensis subsp. madagascariensis
intro: Cordyla madagascariensis subsp. madagascariensis, also known as the 'Madagascar Cordia' or 'Black Bean Tree', is a plant species native to the island of Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its seeds, which contain cordylin, a compound that has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The seeds of Cordyla madagascariensis subsp. madagascariensis have been used to treat digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and powdering the seeds, which can then be used in teas, capsules, or as a topical application to utilize its medicinal properties.
